heading: Competitive leasing of oil and gas
label: 6506a Competitive leasing of oil and gas

Prior to amendment, text related to competitive leasing of oil and gas and consisted of four undesignated pars.Pub. L. 109–58Pub. L. 109–58, § 347(b)(2)1997—, in first par., substituted cls. (8) to (11) and two concluding provisos for “(8) each lease shall be issued for an initial period of up to ten years, and shall be extended for so long thereafter as oil or gas is produced from the lease in paying quantities, or as drilling or reworking operations, as approved by the Secretary, are conducted thereon; and (9) all receipts from sales, rentals, bonuses, and royalties on leases issued pursuant to this Act shall be paid into the Treasury of the United States: , That 50 per centum thereof shall be paid by the Secretary of the Treasury semiannually, as soon as practicable after March 30 and September 30 each year, to the State of Alaska for (a) planning, (b) construction, maintenance, and operation of essential public facilities, and (c) other necessary provisions of public service: , That in the allocation of such funds, the State shall give priority to use by subdivisions of the State most directly or severely impacted by development of oil and gas leased under this Act.”Pub. National Petroleum Reserve–Alaska–(d), , , 144, provided that:Pub. L. 119–21, title V, § 50105(a)139 Stat. 143July 4, 2025“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—NPR–A final environmental impact statementThe term ‘NPR–A final environmental impact statement’ means the final environmental impact statement published by the Bureau of Land Management entitled ‘National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska Integrated Activity Plan Final Environmental Impact Statement’ and dated June 2020, including the errata sheet dated , and excluding the errata sheet dated .October 6, 2020September 20, 2022“(2).—NPR–A record of decisionThe term ‘NPR–A record of decision’ means the record of decision published by the Bureau of Land Management entitled ‘National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska Integrated Activity Plan Record of Decision’ and dated December 2020.“(3).—ProgramThe term ‘Program’ means the competitive oil and gas leasing, exploration, development, and production program established under section 107 of the Naval Petroleum Reserves Production Act of 1976 ().42 U.S.C. 6506a“(4).—SecretaryThe term ‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of the Interior.“(b).—Restoration of NPR–A Oil and Gas Leasing ProgramEffective beginning on the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall expeditiously restore and resume oil and gas lease sales under the Program for domestic energy production and Federal revenue in the areas designated for oil and gas leasing as described in the NPR–A final environmental impact statement and the NPR–A record of decision.July 4, 2025“(c)Resumption of NPR–A Lease Sales.—“(1).—In generalSubject to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall conduct not fewer than 5 lease sales under the Program by not later than 10 years after the date of enactment of this Act.“(2)Sales acreages; schedule.—“(A).—AcreagesIn conducting the lease sales required under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall offer not fewer than 4,000,000 acres in each lease sale.“(B).—ScheduleThe Secretary shall offer—“(i)an initial lease sale under paragraph (1) not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act; and“(ii)an additional lease sale under paragraph (1) not later than every 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act.“(d).—Terms and Stipulations for NPR–A Lease SalesIn conducting lease sales under subsection (c), the Secretary shall offer the same lease form, lease terms, economic conditions, and stipulations as described in the NPR–A final environmental impact statement and the NPR–A record of decision.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct an expeditious program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the Reserve in accordance with this Act.

children.heading: Mitigation of adverse effects
children.label: b Mitigation of adverse effects
children.text: Activities undertaken pursuant to this Act shall include or provide for such conditions, restrictions, and prohibitions as the Secretary deems necessary or appropriate to mitigate reasonably foreseeable and significantly adverse effects on the surface resources of the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska.

children.heading: Land use planning; BLM wilderness study
children.label: c Land use planning; BLM wilderness study
children.text: The provisions of section 1712 and shall not be applicable to the Reserve.section 1782 of title 43

children.heading: First lease sale
children.label: d First lease sale
children.text: The; first lease sale shall be conducted within twenty months of : , That the first lease sale shall be conducted only after publication of a final environmental impact statement if such is deemed necessary under the provisions of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).142 U.S.C. 4321So in original.1December 12, 1980Provided

children.heading: Withdrawals
children.label: e Withdrawals
children.text: The withdrawals established by are rescinded for the purposes of the oil and gas leasing program authorized under this section.section 6502 of this title

children.heading: Bidding systems
children.label: f Bidding systems
children.text: Bidding systems used in lease sales shall be based on bidding systems included in section 205(a)(1)(A) through (H) of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act Amendments of 1978 ().292 Stat. 629See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Geological structures
children.label: g Geological structures
children.text: Lease tracts may encompass identified geological structures.

children.heading: Size of lease tracts
children.label: h Size of lease tracts
children.text: The size of lease tracts may be up to sixty thousand acres, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each lease shall be issued for an initial period of not more than 10 years, and shall be extended for so long thereafter as oil or gas is produced from the lease in paying quantities, oil or gas is capable of being produced in paying quantities, or drilling or reworking operations, as approved by the Secretary, are conducted on the leased land.

children.heading: Renewal of leases with discoveries
children.label: 2 Renewal of leases with discoveries
children.text: At the end of the primary term of a lease the Secretary shall renew for an additional 10-year term a lease that does not meet the requirements of paragraph (1) if the lessee submits to the Secretary an application for renewal not later than 60 days before the expiration of the primary lease and the lessee certifies, and the Secretary agrees, that hydrocarbon resources were discovered on one or more wells drilled on the leased land in such quantities that a prudent operator would hold the lease for potential future development.

children.label: A
children.text: the lessee provides evidence, and the Secretary agrees that, the lessee has diligently pursued exploration that warrants continuation with the intent of continued exploration or future potential development of the leased land; or

children.label: i
children.text: is part of a unit agreement covering a lease described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: has not been previously contracted out of the unit.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 4 Applicability
children.text: This subsection applies to a lease that is in effect on or after .August 8, 2005

children.heading: Expiration for failure to produce
children.label: 5 Expiration for failure to produce
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, if no oil or gas is produced from a lease within 30 years after the date of the issuance of the lease the lease shall expire.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: No lease issued under this section covering lands capable of producing oil or gas in paying quantities shall expire because the lessee fails to produce the same due to circumstances beyond the control of the lessee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of conservation of the natural resources of all or part of any oil or gas pool, field, reservoir, or like area, lessees (including representatives) of the pool, field, reservoir, or like area may unite with each other, or jointly or separately with others, in collectively adopting and operating under a unit agreement for all or part of the pool, field, reservoir, or like area (whether or not any other part of the oil or gas pool, field, reservoir, or like area is already subject to any cooperative or unit plan of development or operation), if the Secretary determines the action to be necessary or advisable in the public interest. In determining the public interest, the Secretary should consider, among other things, the extent to which the unit agreement will minimize the impact to surface resources of the leases and will facilitate consolidation of facilities.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In making a determination under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with and provide opportunities for participation by the State of Alaska or a Regional Corporation (as defined in ) with respect to the creation or expansion of units that include acreage in which the State of Alaska or the Regional Corporation has an interest in the mineral estate.section 1602 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary may use a production allocation methodology for each participating area within a unit that includes solely Federal land in the Reserve.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall use a production allocation methodology for each participating area within a unit that includes Federal land in the Reserve and non-Federal land based on the characteristics of each specific oil or gas pool, field, reservoir, or like area to take into account reservoir heterogeneity and area variation in reservoir producibility across diverse leasehold interests. The implementation of the foregoing production allocation methodology shall be controlled by agreement among the affected lessors and lessees.

children.heading: Benefit of operations
children.label: 4 Benefit of operations
children.text: Drilling, production, and well reworking operations performed in accordance with a unit agreement shall be deemed to be performed for the benefit of all leases that are subject in whole or in part to such unit agreement.

children.heading: Pooling
children.label: 5 Pooling
children.text: If separate tracts cannot be independently developed and operated in conformity with an established well spacing or development program, any lease, or a portion thereof, may be pooled with other lands, whether or not owned by the United States, under a communitization or drilling agreement providing for an apportionment of production or royalties among the separate tracts of land comprising the drilling or spacing unit when determined by the Secretary of the Interior (in consultation with the owners of the other land) to be in the public interest, and operations or production pursuant to such an agreement shall be deemed to be operations or production as to each such lease committed to the agreement.

children.heading: Waiver, suspension, or reduction
children.label: A Waiver, suspension, or reduction
children.text: To encourage the greatest ultimate recovery of oil or gas or in the interest of conservation, the Secretary may waive, suspend, or reduce the rental fees or minimum royalty, or reduce the royalty on an entire leasehold (including on any lease operated pursuant to a unit agreement), whenever (after consultation with the State of Alaska and the North Slope Borough of Alaska and the concurrence of any Regional Corporation for leases that include land that was made available for acquisition by the Regional Corporation under the provisions of section 1431() of the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act) in the judgment of the Secretary it is necessary to do so to promote development, or whenever in the judgment of the Secretary the leases cannot be successfully operated under the terms provided therein.o

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: This paragraph applies to a lease that is in effect on or after ..August 8, 20051

children.heading: Suspension of operations and production
children.label: 2 Suspension of operations and production
children.text: The Secretary may direct or assent to the suspension of operations and production on any lease or unit.

children.heading: Suspension of payments
children.label: 3 Suspension of payments
children.text: If the Secretary, in the interest of conservation, shall direct or assent to the suspension of operations and production on any lease or unit, any payment of acreage rental or minimum royalty prescribed by such lease or unit likewise shall be suspended during the period of suspension of operations and production, and the term of such lease shall be extended by adding any such suspension period to the lease.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), all receipts from sales, rentals, bonuses, and royalties on leases issued pursuant to this section shall be paid into the Treasury of the United States: , That 50 percent thereof shall be paid by the Secretary of the Treasury semiannually, as soon thereafter as practicable after March 30 and September 30 each year, to the State of Alaska for: (1) planning; (2) construction, maintenance, and operation of essential public facilities; and (3) other necessary provisions of public service: , That in the allocation of such funds, the State shall give priority to use by subdivisions of the State most directly or severely impacted by development of oil and gas leased under this Act.ProvidedProvided further

children.heading: Percent share for fiscal year 2034 and thereafter
children.label: 2 Percent share for fiscal year 2034 and thereafter
A
children.text: 70 percent shall be paid to the State of Alaska; and

children.label: B
children.text: 30 percent shall be paid into the Treasury of the United States.

children.heading: Explorations
children.label: m Explorations
children.text: Any agency of the United States and any person authorized by the Secretary may conduct geological and geophysical explorations in the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska which do not interfere with operations under any contract maintained or granted previously. Any information acquired in such explorations shall be subject to the conditions of .43 U.S.C. 1352(a)(1)(A)

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 1 Judicial review
children.text: Any action seeking judicial review of the adequacy of any program or site-specific environmental impact statement under section 102 of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 () concerning oil and gas leasing in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska shall be barred unless brought in the appropriate District Court within 60 days after notice of the availability of such statement is published in the Federal Register.42 U.S.C. 4332

children.heading: Initial lease sales
children.label: 2 Initial lease sales
children.text: The detailed environmental studies and assessments that have been conducted on the exploration program and the comprehensive land-use studies carried out in response to sections 6505(b) and (c) of this title shall be deemed to have fulfilled the requirements of section 102(2)(c) of the National Environmental Policy Act () [], with regard to the first two oil and gas lease sales in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska: , That not more than a total of 2,000,000 acres may be leased in these two sales: , That any exploration or production undertaken pursuant to this section shall be in accordance with .34Public Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4332(2)(C)section 6504(a) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “section”.3So in original. Probably should be “102(2)(C)”.4ProvidedProvided further

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: o Regulations
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall issue regulations to implement this section.August 8, 2005

children.heading: Waiver of administration for conveyed lands
children.label: p Waiver of administration for conveyed lands
A
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior shall waive administration of any oil and gas lease to the extent that the lease covers any land in the Reserve in which all of the subsurface estate is conveyed to the Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (referred to in this subsection as the “Corporation”);

children.label: i
children.text: in a case in which a conveyance of a subsurface estate described in subparagraph (A) does not include all of the land covered by the oil and gas lease, the person that owns the subsurface estate in any particular portion of the land covered by the lease shall be entitled to all of the revenues reserved under the lease as to that portion, including, without limitation, all the royalty payable with respect to oil or gas produced from or allocated to that portion;

children.label: I
children.text: segregate the lease into 2 leases, 1 of which shall cover only the subsurface estate conveyed to the Corporation; and

children.label: II
children.text: waive administration of the lease that covers the subsurface estate conveyed to the Corporation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the segregation of the lease described in clause (ii)(I) has no effect on the obligations of the lessee under either of the resulting leases, including obligations relating to operations, production, or other circumstances (other than payment of rentals or royalties); and

children.label: C
children.text: nothing in this subsection limits the authority of the Secretary of the Interior to manage the federally-owned surface estate within the Reserve.
